text: Four 8mm robotic trocars are inserted, and the robotic system is docked from the patient's left side.
The surgeon begins dissection at the sacral promontory, carefully dissecting the right side of the peritoneum while preserving the autonomic nerves.

The surgeon enters the rectovaginal plane and continues dissection on the anterior side of the mesorectum, reaching the pelvic floor. A vaginal digital examination is performed, and the rectum is mobilized ventrally, dissecting it off the posterior wall of the vagina.
A 10x3 cm polypropylene mesh is inserted into the surgical field. The surgeon uses the first two stitches to anchor the mesh to the posterior vaginal wall.

The surgeon fixes the ventrally mobilized rectum to the sacral promontory using polypropylene mesh secured with three O non-absorbable sutures. The peritoneum is closed with three OV-lock sutures, completing the operation.
The surgeon positions the polypropylene mesh extra-peritoneally to prevent potential complications.
